data.title: Steed Maintains Hold on Balanced Risk-Reward, Cutting Price Target to $80 Despite Solid Q1 Beat and Slight Guidance Raise
data.description:
Analyst Travis Steed from Bank of America Securities reiterated a Hold rating on Merit Medical Systems and decreased the price target to $80.00 from $100.00.

Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Travis Steed has given his Hold rating due to a combination of factors, including a solid first‑quarter beat on revenue, earnings, and margins, along with early evidence that the new segment structure is supporting mid‑single to high‑single‑digit growth across the portfolio. Management also raised full‑year revenue guidance slightly and reiterated the EPS outlook, signaling confidence but maintaining a cautious stance as they typically avoid major guidance changes this early in the year.

At the same time, Steed sees reasons for restraint, such as the modest organic growth outlook, the headwind from the DualCap divestiture, and EPS dilution associated with the View Point acquisition, which together temper the near‑term upside. While he views the View Point deal as strategically attractive for expanding the breast localization addressable market and leveraging the existing sales force, he adjusted his model and cut the price target from $100 to $80, leaving the risk‑reward profile balanced rather than compelling at current levels.

Steed covers the Healthcare sector, focusing on stocks such as Boston Scientific, Stryker, and Medtronic. According to TipRanks, Steed has an average return of 0.2% and a 47.34% success rate on recommended stocks.

In another report released today, Wells Fargo also maintained a Hold rating on the stock with a $74.00 price target.
data.author: TipRanks Auto-Generated Intelligence Newsdesk

data.title: Jeff Schmitt Reiterates Buy as Strong Q1, Commonwealth Synergies and Recruiting Momentum Drive Compelling EPS Growth Outlook Through 2027
data.description:
William Blair analyst Jeff Schmitt has maintained their bullish stance on LPLA stock, giving a Buy rating today.

Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Jeff Schmitt has given his Buy rating due to a combination of factors, including solid first-quarter performance and improving growth visibility. Earnings modestly topped market expectations, with revenue supported by higher client assets from the Commonwealth acquisition and better spread income as client cash balances recover, while expense control and resumed share repurchases enhance profitability.

He also points to a strengthening advisor recruiting pipeline, which should translate into better organic growth in the second half of the year and beyond. Schmitt forecasts roughly 20% average EPS growth in 2026 and 2027, driven by the Commonwealth transaction, accelerating organic growth, higher spread income, ongoing cost discipline, and buybacks, and he notes that shares trade at a low multiple of his 2027 estimate, making the risk-reward compelling.

Schmitt covers the Financial sector, focusing on stocks such as Tradeweb Markets, Marketaxess Holdings, and Nasdaq. According to TipRanks, Schmitt has an average return of 8.6% and a 54.13% success rate on recommended stocks.
data.author: TipRanks Auto-Generated Intelligence Newsdesk

data.title: FLSmidth & Co. A/S: Buy Rating and $610 Price Target Reaffirmed Despite Russia-Related Sanctions Overhang
data.description:
Analyst David Farrell of Jefferies maintained a Buy rating on FLSmidth & Co. A/S, retaining the price target of DKK610.00.

Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

David Farrell has given his Buy rating due to a combination of factors, despite the announcement of a potential breach of Russia-related sanctions tied to tender activity in Kazakhstan. He notes that Kazakhstan represents only a minor contribution to FLSmidth & Co. A/S’s overall business, and the company’s latest large original equipment order from that market has already been delivered, limiting direct financial impact.

Farrell also emphasizes that there is no change to the firm’s 2026 financial outlook or to its intention to start a share buyback in the second quarter, which signals management confidence in the underlying business. While he acknowledges that the issue may weigh on investor sentiment and slightly dampen order prospects, he views it mainly as a reputational overhang rather than a fundamental threat, and therefore keeps his price target unchanged at $610, supporting his Buy recommendation.

In another report released on April 20, RBC Capital also maintained a Buy rating on the stock with a DKK610.00 price target.
data.author: TipRanks Auto-Generated Intelligence Newsdesk
